highlights.title: Mid-Century Architecture
highlights.body: Home to thousands of Kaufmann Desert House–era masterpieces by Richard Neutra, Albert Frey, and Donald Wexler. Modernism Week draws 150,000 architecture lovers annually.

highlights.title: Palm Springs Aerial Tramway
highlights.body: The world's largest rotating tram car whisks you from 2,643 ft to 8,516 ft in minutes — desert heat to pine forest, year-round hiking, and breathtaking views.

highlights.title: World-Class Arts Scene
highlights.body: From the Palm Springs Art Museum to monthly VillageFest street fairs, the city pulses with creativity. Public art, galleries, and cultural festivals are woven into daily life.

highlights.title: Golf & Outdoor Recreation
highlights.body: Dozens of golf courses surround the city, with Indian Canyons and hiking trails steps away. Year-round sunshine makes every outdoor activity possible every day.

highlights.title: El Paseo Shopping
highlights.body: The "Rodeo Drive of the Desert" stretches a mile of luxury boutiques, art galleries, sidewalk cafés, and fine dining — all framed by mountain views and palm trees.

highlights.title: McCallum Theatre
highlights.body: The Coachella Valley's premier performing arts venue hosts Broadway productions, world-class musicians, and the acclaimed Off the Wall series — a true cultural anchor.

highlights.title: Premier Golf
highlights.body: Desert Falls Country Club, Bighorn Golf Club, and The Reserve rank among the valley's most coveted fairways — all within Palm Desert's limits.

highlights.title: College of the Desert
highlights.body: A vibrant community college campus hosting farmers markets, enrichment programs, and contributing to Palm Desert's energetic, educated community identity.

highlights.title: Coachella Music Festival
highlights.body: Every April, Empire Polo Club hosts one of the world's greatest music and arts festivals. As a resident, you're steps away from a global cultural phenomenon — and the rental income potential is extraordinary.

highlights.title: Stagecoach Festival
highlights.body: The country music counterpart to Coachella draws massive crowds and creates robust short-term rental demand. Two major annual festivals generate rental revenue for owners.

highlights.title: Date Palm Heritage
highlights.body: Indio is the Date Capital of the U.S. Shield Date Garden and Oasis Date Gardens have cultivated date palms since the early 20th century — a living piece of California agricultural history.

highlights.title: Fantasy Springs Resort Casino
highlights.body: A full-resort casino with hotel, concerts, dining, and gaming — adding significant entertainment infrastructure to Indio's eastern corridor.

highlights.title: Natural Hot Springs
highlights.body: Natural mineral water from the Miracle Hill Aquifer feeds dozens of spas and hotels. Residents enjoy therapeutic soaking year-round — a natural amenity found nowhere else in the valley.

highlights.title: 360° Mountain Views
highlights.body: Sitting above the valley floor, Desert Hot Springs offers unobstructed panoramas of the San Jacinto, San Gorgonio, and Little San Bernardino mountain ranges.

highlights.title: Joshua Tree Proximity
highlights.body: The northern gateway to Joshua Tree National Park is just 20 minutes away — easy access to one of America's most spectacular parks for hiking, stargazing, and weekend adventures.

highlights.title: Cooler Desert Climate
highlights.body: At 1,065 feet — significantly higher than the valley floor — Desert Hot Springs enjoys average temperatures 10°F cooler than Palm Springs. Summer evenings are noticeably more comfortable.

highlights.title: Sunnylands
highlights.body: The Annenberg estate hosts world leaders, public garden tours, and cultural programming on 200 immaculate acres — a rare cultural anchor unique to Rancho Mirage.

highlights.title: Ultimate Privacy
highlights.body: Some of the valley's most established guard-gated communities, celebrity residences, and discreet luxury — the kind of address that doesn't broadcast itself.

highlights.title: Mission Hills & Tamarisk
highlights.body: Two of the desert's most celebrated golf clubs. Plus Eisenhower Medical Center, the valley's flagship medical campus, sits within city limits.

highlights.title: BNP Paribas Open
highlights.body: The 'fifth Grand Slam.' Two weeks every March, the world's tennis elite plays at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden — and residents get year-round resident discounts on facilities.

highlights.title: Indian Wells Golf Resort
highlights.body: Two acclaimed courses (Players & Celebrity) with resident programs that make this one of the best golf-living deals in California.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated Living
highlights.body: Nearly every neighborhood is guard-gated. Indian Wells is consistently ranked among the wealthiest cities in California, with strict architectural controls preserving the character.

highlights.title: PGA West
highlights.body: Six championship courses including the famed TPC Stadium — host of multiple PGA Tour events. The La Quinta Resort built the desert's golf-vacation reputation a century ago.

highlights.title: La Quinta Resort & Spa
highlights.body: A landmark since 1926, with adobe-style casitas spread across 45 acres of grounds. Hollywood's original desert escape.

highlights.title: Master-Planned Communities
highlights.body: Trilogy, Andalusia, The Hideaway, The Madison Club, and others — among the valley's most amenity-rich, fastest-appreciating master-planned developments.

highlights.title: Cathedral City Cove
highlights.body: Tucked into the foothills with hillside lots, mountain views, and the most affordable single-family entry point adjacent to Palm Springs.

highlights.title: Vibrant Arts Scene
highlights.body: Public art installations, cultural festivals, and a growing dining scene make Cathedral City one of the valley's emerging design destinations.

highlights.title: Inclusive Community
highlights.body: Cathedral City has long led the valley in community inclusivity, with active LGBTQ+ residents and diverse cultural programming.

highlights.title: Growing Community
highlights.body: Coachella is the Coachella Valley's most affordable city and one of its fastest-growing — significant new construction is reshaping its housing landscape.

highlights.title: Festival Proximity
highlights.body: Closest residential city to the Empire Polo Club festival grounds. Coachella, Stagecoach, and other event seasons make short-term rentals especially compelling.

highlights.title: Lake Cahuilla
highlights.body: Lake Cahuilla Veterans Regional Park — fishing, camping, hiking — sits at the city's edge. A rare natural amenity in this part of the valley.
